title: ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES - 211info
text: CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers alcohol and drug treatment services through a network of providers. Services also may be available for mandated treatment as the result of a DUII. Services include information and education, as well as treatment for substance use disorder issues. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults and youth with substance use issues Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 742-5335 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 742-5335 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and referral FEES: Accepts OHP and most private insurance. State General Fund for individuals who are uninsured. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus lines 32, 33 Services: Alcohol Use Disorder Education/Prevention Assessment for Substance Use Disorders Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment Drug Use Disorder Education/Prevention DUI Offender Programs Substance Use Disorder Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - 24/7 MOBILE CRISIS RESPONSE TEAM MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CLACKAMAS COUNTY CRISIS AND SUPPORT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION
